2606.14828.title: Leptomeningeal Collateral Detection on DSA via Vessel-Graph Neural Networks
2606.14828.paper_summary: Leptomeningeal collaterals (LMCs) are an important prognostic factor in acute ischemic stroke. Existing automated methods rely on CT angiography (CTA), but individual LMCs are often too small to be resolved on CTA, limiting these methods to coarse collateral scoring. Digital subtraction angiography (DSA) visualizes individual collaterals at superior resolution, yet current assessment remains subjective, relying on manual grading scales that suffer from poor inter-rater agreement. We present a framework that formulates collateral detection as the classification of individual vessel segments on a graph derived from DSA. A hybrid graph-pixel architecture combines a topology-aware graph branch with a dense pixel branch, fused in a shared node-probability space. In a five-fold cross-validation setting, the fused model achieves a PR-AUC of 0.434, outperforming the graph-only (0.403) and pixel-only (0.362) baselines. To our knowledge, this is the first method to enable the individualization of LMCs in DSA, allowing for precise per-vessel quantitative assessment. This integration shifts DSA assessment toward objective evaluation, supporting future biomarker and pattern discovery for individual LMCs.
2606.14828.paper_summary_zh: Leptomeningeal collaterals (LMCs) 是急性缺血性中風的重要預後因素。現有的自動化方法依賴於 CT 血管造影 (CTA)，但個別的 LMCs 通常太小，無法在 CTA 上解析，限制了這些方法的粗略側支評分。數位減影血管造影 (DSA) 以更高的解析度可視化個別側支，但目前的評估仍然是主觀的，依賴於手動評分標準，這些標準的評分者之間一致性差。我們提出一個框架，將側支檢測公式化為從 DSA 衍生的圖上個別血管段的分類。一個混合圖-像素架構將一個考慮拓撲的圖分支與一個密集的像素分支結合，在共享的節點-概率空間中融合。在五折交叉驗證設置中，融合模型達到 0.434 的 PR-AUC，超越了僅圖形 (0.403) 和僅像素 (0.362) 的基準。據我們所知，這是第一種能夠在 DSA 中實現 LMCs 個體化的方法，允許對每個血管進行精確的定量評估。這種整合將 DSA 評估轉向客觀評價，支持未來對個別 LMCs 的生物標記和模式發現。
2606.14828.author: Junyong Cao et.al.
2606.14828.authors: Junyong Cao, Hakim Baazaoui, Chinmay Prabhakar, Suprosanna Shit, Lukas Bastian Otto, Susanne Wegener, Bjoern Menze, Ezequiel de la Rosa
